MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. DEMIAN I. OKSENENDLER OF THE MANNION & LOWE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-733-1054 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ANTHONY DIPIETRA OF THE GLADSTONE WEISBERG ALC, TEL. NO. 1-972-881-1473 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - The motion is denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to maintain its initial summary judgment/summary adjudication burden. Plaintiff may be able to establish the breach of contract and bad faith claims. The evidence indicates that Plaintiff's walnut tree may have been covered, but defendant never investigated that portion of the damage. Defendant's PMK regarding claims handling admitted that the tree was not properly investigated. (Guerra Depo., 131:25-135:13). The court denies summary adjudication as to the remaining claims. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Wang was acting as Defendant's agent. "The most definitive characteristic of an insurance agent is his authority to bind his principal, the insurer; an insurance broker has no such authority." Marsh & McLennan of Cal., Inc. v City of LA (1976) 62 Cal.App.3d 108, 117. Here, Mr. Wang testified that he used his binding authority to procure Plaintiff's policy from Defendant. (Paul Wang Depo., 280:3-9; 282:11-19; 283:7-21). Even Defendant admits that Wang had such binding authority. (Smith Dec., par. 15). If there is an agency relationship, then Defendant is vicariously liable for Wang's misconduct/negligence. It is possible that Wang was acting as a dual agent and Kurtz, Richards, Wilson & Co. v. Ins. Communicators Marketing Corp. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1249, 1257, notes that such relationships are not uncommon. The bad faith claim based on a reformed policy is viable because coverage was available under the original or reformed version of the policy. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Demian I. Oksenendler, of Mannion & Lowe APC, Tel. 415.733.1050, for plaintiffs;
Melissa Grant, of Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP, Tel. 415.217.6990, for defendants;
Anthony DiPietra, of Gladstone Weisberg, ALC, Tel. 310.821.9000, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT WANG INSURANCE AGENCY, INC., SIMON GIANG'S Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint is denied. The cause of the fire and the issues in plaintiff's complaint (bad faith, negligence) involve distinct transactions. Moreover, granting the motion will lead to a trial continuance. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Defendant California Capital Insurance Company'S Motion To Compel Answers To Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel In The Amount Of $2,875
Off Calendar May-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant California Capital Insurance Company's Motion To Compel Answers To Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel In The Amount Of $2,875 is Granted. The objection that the interrogatories are compound is overruled. The special interrogatories may technically be compound in their precise language, but they reflect an inquiry to a single subject. (See Clement v. Alegre (2009) 117 Cal.App.4th 1277.) Defendant's timely service of the motion and timely attempt to file the motion with the Clerk of Court who initially rejected filing of the pleadings based on a Local Rule which conflicts with California Rule of Court 3.1112, is not a basis for the Court to not consider the motion on the merits. The request for sanctions is denied. Order signed. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR # 5963 =302/sb

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. First, the documents submitted with Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice fail to disprove that California Insurance Group ("CIG") is a legal entity, and there are sufficient allegations in the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") to establish a basis for CIG's liability. Second, Defendants inherently acknowledge that, because the insurance policy was not attached to the FAC, it is not apparent from the face of the Complaint that CIG was not a party to the contract. Thus, Defendants' failure to request judicial notice of the policy forecloses a demurrer on this grounds as a demurrer can be used only to challenge defects appearing on the face of the pleading or from matters that are properly judicially noticed. Next, the FAC states a valid cause of action for negligence by sufficiently alleging the wrongful conduct of the broker and that the broker was acting as an agent of Defendants. (See Desai v. Farmers Ins. Exchange (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1110 [holding that if an agent fails to exercise reasonable care in procuring the type of insurance that the insured demanded and bargained for, the insurer may be liable].) Additionally, the FAC states valid causes of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentation (see FAC Paragraphs 5, 68, 76, 78-79), and the allegations are not uncertain. Finally, Defendants' attempt to specially demur to the third and fourth causes of action are overruled. (See CRC 3.1113(a) [providing for waiver of all grounds of special demurrers not supported in the memorandum of points and authorities].) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Continued To Jan-07-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS WANG INSURANCE AGENCY, INC. and SIMON GIANG'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint (FAC) sufficiently alleges a basis for an award of attorneys' fees. (See Prentice v. North Amer. Title Guar. Corp (1963) 59 Cal.2d 618, 620-21.) The FAC alleges causes of action against the Insurance Defendants for breach of contract and reformation, which Plaintiff has been required to pursue in order to protect his interests as a result of Defendants' alleged negligence, which is a sufficient basis for seeking relief pursuant to the tort of another doctrine. (See id.) Furthermore, the relief sought is not precluded merely because Plaintiff also asserts claims for negligence, fraud, and misrepresentation against the Insurance Defendants, nor due to the allegations that all defendants may be liable as joint tortfeasors. It is well established in California that "a party may plead in the alternative and may make inconsistent allegations." (Third Eye Blind, Inc. v. Near North Entertainment Ins. Services, LLC (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 1311, 1323.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS WANG INSURANCE AGENCY, INC. and SIMON GIANG'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. The Motion to Strike the punitive damages is denied. The Complaint alleges a valid cause of action for fraud. The Motion is granted with 15 days leave to amend as to the request for attorneys' fees to permit Plaintiff to plead facts that support a claim under the doctrine of tort of another. The Court signed the proposed order that was submitted by email. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS WANG INSURANCE AGENCY, INC. and SIMON GIANG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled as to the cause of action for breach of contract as Plaintiff sufficiently pled the legal effect of the contract. (Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 199; Khoury v. Maly's of Cal. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) Demurrer is also overruled as to the fraud and negligent misrepresentation causes of action because the Complaint sufficiently alleges each required element for both causes of action. The Court signed the proposed order that was submitted by email. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
